zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel St Leger於1319年5月19日出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可利其哈姆時，她的父親John St Leger26歲，母親Joan de Baddington25歲。她於1335年6月16日與Sir Thomas de Hoo在英國貝德福爾盧頓 胡結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1393年7月12日在英國英格蘭素可海爾善姆享年74歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel St Leger于1319年5月19日出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可利其哈姆时，她的父亲John St Leger26岁，母亲Joan de Baddington25岁。她于1335年6月16日与Sir Thomas de Hoo在英国貝德福爾盧頓 胡结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1393年7月12日在英国英格兰素可海爾善姆享年74岁，埋葬在英国英格兰賀特福爾聖 阿爾班斯。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Leger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Lucien, Pierre, Yvon, Antoine, Aurele, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Zoel, Adelard.

French (Léger) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Leudgari, composed of the elements leud ‘people’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’ and Latinized as Leodegar(ius). The name was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, bishop of Autun, whose fame contributed to the popularity of the name in France. See also Ledger and St. Leger .
French (Léger): nickname from léger ‘light, superficial’.

variants.name: Lege
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphie, Emile, Gaston, Henri, Leonce, Minus, Numa, Pierre, Rodolph.
German: topographic name meaning ‘swamp’.
Altered form of French Léger (see Leger ).

variants.name: Legere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Armand, Normand, Aurele, Jacques, Adelard, Alban, Antoine, Desire, Eloi, Monique, Yvon.
French (Légère): nickname from légère, a feminine form of Léger ‘light, superficial’ (see Leger 2).
Altered form of French   Leger   1 or 2.

variants.name: Lougee
meanings: Channel Islands: altered form of Laugee, a variant of French Lauger, itself either a variant of Léger (see Leger ) or a variant, with fused definite article l', of Auger .

History: The first recorded American bearer of this surname was John Lougee from the isle of Jersey. Born Jean Laugee and by trade a knitter, he was aged c. 18 or 19 when he emigrated to the USA in the late 1600s, settling in Exeter, NH, where he became known as John Lougee, marrying Anne, the daughter of Colonel Moses Gilman.

variants.name: Ledger
meanings: English: from a Norman personal name, Leodegar, Old French Legier, of ancient Germanic origin (see Leger 1).
English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English legger ‘one who lays stone, a mason’, a derivative of Old English lecgan ‘to lay’.
Americanized form of French   Leger   1.

variants.name: St. Leger
meanings: French (Saint-Léger): habitational name from any of several places in various parts of France named Saint-Léger (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Leodegar, in French St. Léger, the martyred 7th-century bishop of Autun), or a nickname with the prefix Saint as an (ironic) attachment to the personal name or surname Léger (see Leger 1).

Haitian (also Saint-Léger): from Saint-Léger, a nickname, ornamental name, or personal name composed of the French prefix Saint and the personal name Léger (see Leger 1), or perhaps a habitational name from a place in Haiti called Saint-Léger (compare 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Léger in La Manche or Saint-Léger-aux-Bois in Seine-Maritime, France (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel St Leger was born on 19 May 1319, in Litcham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, John St Leger, was 26 and her mother, Joan de Baddington, was 25. She married Sir Thomas de Hoo on 16 June 1335, in Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 12 July 1393, in Hailsham, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74, and was buried in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel St Leger est née le 19 mai 1319, à Litcham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John St Leger, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Joan de Baddington, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas de Hoo le 16 juin 1335, à Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 12 juillet 1393, à Hailsham, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 74 ans, et a été enterrée à St Albans, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel St Legerが1319年5月19日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクリトチャムで生まれた時，父John St Legerは26歳，母Joan de Baddingtonは25歳であった。1335年6月16日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアルトン ホーオでSir Thomas de Hooと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1393年7月12日にイギリスイギリススセックスヘイルシャムで74歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスハートフォードシュアイアスト アルバンスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel St Leger(이)가 1319년 5월 19일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 리트챔에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John St Leger(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Baddington(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1335년 6월 16일 영국 베드퍼드실르 루턴 후에서 Sir Thomas de Hoo(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1393년 7월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 하이샤에서 74세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 허트퍼드실르 스트 알반스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel St Leger nasceu em 19 de maio de 1319, em Litcham, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John St Leger, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Baddington, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas de Hoo em 16 de junho de 1335, em Luton Hoo, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel St Leger родилась 19 мая 1319 г. в местности личам, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John St Leger было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Baddington, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas de Hoo 16 июня 1335 г. в местности лутон ху, бедфордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 12 июля 1393 г. в местности хеилшам, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет и была похоронена в местности ст албанс, хиртфордшир, Англия, Великобритания.
